jobs.basic_qualifications:

jobs.company_name: Amazon Web Services Korea LLC - D54
jobs.description:
The Amazon Web Services Professional Services (ProServe) team is seeking a skilled Senior Delivery Consultant with telecommunications industry specialization to join our team. In this role, you'll work closely with telecommunications customers to design, implement, and manage AWS solutions that meet their technical requirements and business objectives. You'll be a key player in driving customer success through their cloud journey, providing both strategic technical leadership and hands-on delivery expertise throughout the project lifecycle.

As a Senior Delivery Consultant specializing in telecommunications, you will possess deep understanding of AWS products and services, with specific expertise in telecom industry challenges including cloud and virtualized network functions, BSS, OSS, Private 5G, Public 5G, Multi Access Edge (MEC), Enterprise IT applications, and data residency requirements. You'll combine strategic advisory capabilities with hands-on technical implementation skills.

Key job responsibilities
Technical Leadership & Solution Design:

Design and implement complex, scalable, and secure AWS solutions tailored to telecommunications customer needs
Lead technical and design discussions with telecom IT executives to help enterprises speed their adoption of new technologies and practices
Architect cloud-native telecommunications solutions leveraging AWS services for network functions, edge computing, and 5G infrastructure
Guide customers' technical investments, maximizing alignment with the AWS platform and ease of adoption as new services become available
Build solutions that solve difficult technical problems to accomplish defined business outcomes for telecommunications customers

Hands-on Technical Implementation & Delivery:

Hands-on implementation of Infrastructure as Code (IaC), configuration management for telecom infrastructure and applications
Build and manage release systems, code merging and promotion, and CI/CD workflows for telecom workloads
Architect and build highly secure scalable cloud native solutions for telecommunications customers
Implement DevOps practices such as infrastructure as code, continuous integration and automated deployment for telecom environments
Build and deploy automation solutions for telecommunications operations and network management

Customer Engagement & Advisory:

Act as a trusted advisor to telecommunications customers on industry trends, emerging technologies, and innovative cloud solutions
Work with customers and partners to internalize their context while using business and technical skills to design solutions based on requirements and constraints
Lead customer discovery sessions that earn trust, uncover blockers, and document technical scope for telecom-specific workloads
Provide technical guidance and troubleshooting support throughout project delivery
Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements, assess current telecom infrastructure, and propose effective migration strategies to AWS

Project Delivery & Implementation:

Manage projects independently and deliver as part of larger teams focused on telecom transformations
Own and complete key tasks and deliverables for telecommunications cloud adoption initiatives
Ensure adherence to best practices, optimize performance, and manage risks throughout telecom project lifecycles
Work towards customer business outcomes, ensuring strong connection between delivery activities and telecom business objectives
Lead hands-on implementation of telecom-specific AWS solutions including network functions virtualization and edge computing

Automation & DevOps Leadership:

Hands-on experience with architecting and automated provisioning using CloudFormation, Terraform, or other IaC tools
Implement monitoring and log aggregating frameworks for telecom workloads
Lead automation initiatives for telecommunications operations and network management
Advise and implement AWS best security practices for telecom environments

Knowledge Sharing & Innovation:

Create and apply frameworks, methods, best practices and artifacts specific to telecommunications industry
Share knowledge within the organization through mentoring, training, and creating reusable telecom-focused artifacts
Publish and present telecom cloud solutions in large forums and across various media platforms
Collaborate with AWS product teams to share telecom customer insights and identify product feature gaps
Keep up to date with new DevOps trends and technologies relevant to telecommunications

jobs.company_name: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
jobs.description:
Forefront of helping customers leverage Accelerated Computing instances, including Trainium/Inferentia, GPU-based workloads, and High Performance Computing (HPC) solutions to solve their most complex business challenges.

As a specialist, you'll accelerate the adoption and consumption of Accelerated Compute (XC) services through strategic go-to-market initiatives, co-sell partnerships, sales enablement, and technical support. You'll work backwards from customers to develop and execute GTM strategies that drive innovation in AI/ML, HPC, graphics, and gaming workloads, reinforcing AWS as the best place for customers' Accelerated Compute needs.

Your mission will encompass three key focus areas: leading GTM strategies for Accelerated Compute services, creating leverage through scalable sales plays and field enablement, and shaping the evolution of AWS services by gathering customer insights and market dynamics to influence product roadmaps. You'll collaborate as #OneTeam with AWS Sales, Marketing, Global Services, and Partners to deliver results while providing closed-loop feedback to AWS Service teams.

This is an opportunity to work with leading technology, engage with innovative customers across ANZ, and make a significant impact on how organizations adopt accelerated computing to transform their businesses. You'll be part of a global team of domain experts who provide specialist expertise across the entire lifecycle of AWS initiatives, from developing new service strategies to accelerating the adoption of established offerings.

Key job responsibilities

- Develop and execute comprehensive go-to-market plans for Accelerated Compute services including Trainium/Inferentia, GPU instances, and HPC workloads across ANZ;
- Drive customer adoption through strategic engagements, technical workshops, and executive briefings that demonstrate the value of AWS Accelerated Compute solutions;
- Build and maintain deep relationships with key customers, understanding their workload requirements and translating them into actionable AWS solutions;
- Create repeatable sales plays and enablement materials that help scale successful customer patterns across the field sales organization
- Collaborate with Solution Architects, Account Managers, and Partners to identify opportunities and remove technical blockers for customer success
- Aggregate customer capacity requirements and work with EC2 product teams to ensure regional availability meets market demand;
- Provide technical and business expertise to support complex customer migrations to Accelerated Compute instances;
- Gather customer insights, use case feedback, and market trends to influence AWS service roadmaps and feature development;
- Enable field teams, partners, and professional services through training, technical content creation, and thought leadership;
- Track and report on key metrics including pipeline generation, customer adoption, and revenue impact of specialist engagements;


A day in the life
You'll start your day reviewing customer engagements and coordinating with account teams on strategic opportunities requiring Accelerated Compute expertise. You might conduct a technical deep-dive session with a Financial Services customer exploring GPU instances for risk modeling, followed by a partner enablement workshop on Trainium for AI/ML workloads. Afternoons could involve collaborating with the worldwide XC team on capacity planning, contributing to a sales play for HPC in life sciences, or providing technical guidance to help close a significant deal. You'll also dedicate time to staying current with the latest AWS innovations, creating customer-facing content, and sharing insights with product teams to shape future service offerings.

About the team
The ANZ Accelerated Compute Specialist team is part of the broader AWS Worldwide Specialist Organization (WWSO), working closely with Area Specialist teams across APJ. We're a customer-obsessed team that combines deep technical expertise with strong business acumen to drive meaningful outcomes. Our team culture emphasizes collaboration, innovation, and continuous learning. We work as #OneTeam with sales, solutions architecture, partners, and professional services to maximize customer success and AWS growth in the region.

jobs.company_name: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
jobs.description:
AWS Specialist Solutions Architects (SSAs) are technologists with deep domain-specific expertise, able to address advanced concepts and feature designs. As part of the AWS sales organization, SSAs work with customers who have complex challenges that require expert-level knowledge to solve. SSAs craft scalable, flexible, and resilient technical architectures that address those challenges. This might involve guiding customers as they refactor an application or designing entirely new cloud-based systems. Specialist SAs play a critical role in capturing customer feedback, advocating for roadmap enhancements, and anticipating customer requirements as they work backwards from their needs. As domain experts, SSAs also participate in field engagement and enablement, producing content such as blogs and workshops for customers, partners, and the AWS Technical Field.

Data analytics SSAs combine technical skills in data, analytics, AIML with business acumen, and technical sales skills. They are focused on supporting customers throughout the engagement lifecycle and work closely with the teams that support implementation including AWS Professional Services and Partners. SSAs unblock customers and help them get to production faster. Joining AWS as a Specialist Solutions Architect will give you opportunities to work on interesting and challenging problems, collaborate with a group of skilled practitioners focused on delivering outcomes for our customers, and be part of a diverse and inclusive community committed to being Earth’s Best Employer.

Specialist Solutions Architects are part of the Amazon Global Sales Organization (AGS) which builds and executes go-to-market plans that turn AWS ideas into multi-billion-dollar businesses. Our teams include business development, specialist sales, and specialist solutions architecture, working together to provide customer-obsessed solutions that help people run their businesses more effectively. We pride ourselves on thinking big, delivering exceptional results for our customers, and working across AWS as #OneTeam

Key job responsibilities
Design best in class data analytics solutions for all stages of customer and product lifecycles.
Partner with SAs, Sales, Business Development and Service teams to accelerate customer adoption and revenue attainment.
Thought Leadership - Evangelize and share best practices through forums such as AWS blogs, reference architectures and public-speaking events such as AWS Summit, AWS re: Invent, etc.
Act as a technical liaison between customers and the AWS teams to provide customer driven product improvement feedback.
Develop and support an AWS internal community of related subject matter experts.

A day in the life
Watch this video to find out what it’s like to be a Solutions Architect at AWS?

About the team
This role is part of the Data and AI specialist team for Australia and New Zealand. As a member of this team you will work with customers and be based out of our Auckland offices. You will focus on customers across New Zealand, working directly with customers across industry verticals, from large Enterprises to start-ups, across all stages of their cloud journey.

jobs.description:
Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager. In this role, you will lead a team of hourly workforce and will be responsible for engaging your team during their shifts to maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance. You will also play a key role in maintaining our customer expectations to ensure customer orders are delivered at the right time, to the right location.

Our Operation’s workflow has three major components: First mile - where the product is housed and ready for order; Middle mile - where the order is hauled to your area; and Last mile - when the product is delivered to the customer’s door. Please note that all workflows have slight building variations, but one thing is constant: our vision and dedication to the customer.

Key job responsibilities
* Support, mentor, and motivate your hourly workforce
* Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises
* Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives
* Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
* Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
* Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud
* Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)
* Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)

Our fulfillment network launches new Operations sites every year, providing various opportunities for your professional growth. We hire Area Managers based on location preference and the business’ current openings.
jobs.description_short: Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager.

- Bachelor's degree in Architecture, Construction Management, Engineering, or a related field
- Experience as an Architect, Engineer, Construction Manager, Development Manager, Owner's project management representative or in other Commercial Development and Construction related disciplines
jobs.description:
The Global Real Estate and Facilities (GREF) team provides real estate transaction expertise, business partnering, space & occupancy planning, design and construction, capital investment program management and facility maintenance and operations for Amazon’s corporate office portfolio across multiple countries. We partner with suppliers to ensure quality, innovation, and operational excellence with Amazon’s business and utilize customer driven feedback to continuously improve and exceed employee expectations.

GREF’s Workplace Design and Construction team is looking for a Pre-Construction Manager to drive construction services on projects, including tenant improvements, new builds, expansions, and renovations of Amazon corporate facilities. Responsible for delivery of projects from early ideation through design and procurement, this individual must be comfortable operating on both strategic and tactical levels, simultaneously acting as an internal ambassador, educator, analyst, solutions architect, and implementer. This role collaborates with leadership and other stakeholders to set a budget and schedule, then drive project objectives, scope, space allocation, cost, prioritization, and sequencing of requests all within the set parameters. All of this is accomplished by building strong design and construction project teams that thrive and excel through collaboration. The role also involves managing vendor/contractor performance to ensure timely completion, compliance with cost requirements, organizational standards, and other requirements and regulations.


Key job responsibilities
o Represent GREF to internal customers for the delivery of their space.

o Work closely with internal and external stakeholders, including transaction managers, portfolio managers, design managers, architects, design consultants, contractors, and subcontractors, to ensure decisions are made at the appropriate level and Amazon's priorities remain the driving factor. Lead constructive dialogue to resolve and prevent project issues.

o You are focused on scope development with internal stakeholders to develop the project charter for design direction. You keep the scope under control and accelerate progress by driving crisp and timely decisions, identifying and clearing blockers, and escalating appropriately.

o Develop estimates, monitor project budgets, and prepare forecasts. Identify and mitigate project risks, ensure compliance with contracts and regulations, and resolve project issues.

o You understand design and construction contracts, exhibits, and provide detailed analysis and approvals of technical matters.

o Review and advise on landlord delivery conditions in LOI and lease documents, including premises delivery timing and condition.

o Provide management of design and construction projects, which may include the following:
• Develop project strategies, lead vendor procurement, and ensure appropriate resources are allocated to complete projects in accordance with strategies.
• Lead project meetings and progress reviews.
• Evaluate and review design changes proposed by customers with design managers.


A day in the life
Each day you will represent GREF in ensuring the on time and in budget execution of Amazon construction projects, while strategically improving processes. Daily tasks may comprise of construction coordination management and design document development with various consultants on projected changes, project status, ensuring consultants are meeting contractual obligations, and utilizing the Amazon information systems to record and update scheduling, budget and other key developments. Each day you will be navigating through uncertainty, proactively unblocking project barriers and influencing the best project decisions. You will be negotiating terms of contracts with developers, contractors and consultants all the while driving to meet or exceed schedules and budgets of the approved scope.

Daily tasks may be comprised of stakeholder management, coordinating with internal teams/vendors/contractors on projected changes and project status, ensuring vendors and contractors are meeting their contractual obligations, seeking sustainable environmentally friendly solutions throughout the design and construction process while implementing Amazon’s building environmentally friendly practices, and utilizing the Amazon information systems to record and update scheduling, budget, and other key developments. You may be negotiating terms of contracts with designers and contractors all the while driving them to meet or exceed agreed-to schedules and budgets.

You will represent Amazon’s interests in weekly architect, consultants, and contractor meetings providing input on root causes and corrective action. You will lead multiple medium sized to large sized projects of varying sizes and functions. You will be tactical and strategic working independently, interacting with leadership to resolve issues. You will provide estimating support, interpret drawings, contribute to budgets and approve lease exhibits. Pre-construction management also includes site validation due diligence, design management and validation of checklists and construction documentation. For jurisdictional coordination you will understand zoning/planning, fire code and building code. You will lead large cross functional teams establishing confidence in the Amazon business and architecture community. You act as the primary escalation point for project team members; owning and tracking issues to ensure that they are addressed, documented, and resolved using influence to manage team members.

About the team

At Amazon, we are all owners and leverage unique opportunities presented to us by owning everything from the design review, permitting to construction bidding and consultant during construction execution prior to final hand-off to our internal stakeholder. We are a diverse, upbeat, creative, team of designers, engineers and managers working daily to develop innovative sustainable facilities and efficient innovative buildings for our customers. We focus on Delivering Results, our speed for Bias for Action and Insisting on the Highest Standards for our customers.
jobs.description_short: The Global Real Estate and Facilities (GREF) team provides real estate transaction expertise, business partnering, space & occupancy planning, design and construction, capital investment program management

- Demonstrated capability in the planning, design review & implementation, and project management /control of new structures & the enhancement of existing facility buildings.

jobs.description:
Would you like to be part of the top growing team in AWS Korea to drive the adoption of AWS services with MEGS team? Do you have the business savvy and technical literacy necessary to work with businesses large and small?

AWS is seeking an experienced sales professional to help manage the customer relationship in MEGS industry. As an Sr. Account Manager, you will have the exciting opportunity to help drive the growth and shape the future of Cloud Computing adoption within the most dynamic companies in Korea. Your responsibilities will include communicating with the customer’s leadership team, with the goals of maximizing customer success and customer satisfaction.

Key job responsibilities
- Drive cloud usage growth and market share in the assigned territory
- Meet or exceed quarterly business goals.
- Develop and execute against a comprehensive account/territory plan.
- Create & articulate compelling value propositions around AWS services.
- Work with partners to extend reach & drive adoption.
- Manage and support customer interactions
- Develop long-term strategic relationships with key accounts.
- Ensure customer satisfaction.
- The account manager will closely work with customers in MEGS to promote AWS services in a defined territory or industry vertical and help customer to adopt cloud as a trusted advisor.
- The account should maintain a robust opportunity pipeline and work with partners to extend reach & promote adoption.
- The account manager should orchestrate resources for executions in center of engagement and work with Solutions Architect, Business Development Manager, Professional Service, Partner Manager, and necessarily global service team to execute plan.

A day in the life
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

About the team
Korea MEGS Team

The Korea MEGS team is responsible for driving revenue, adoption, and growth from the major Korean MEGS customers. We are helping the customers innovate and grow their own business based on cloud computing and AI.

- Knowledge of MS Excel, Word, and Windows Operating Systems
- Experience in engineering including a supervisory role managing people

jobs.description:
At Amazon, we're working to be the most customer-centric company on earth. To get there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people. If you'd like to help us build the place to find and buy anything online, this is your chance to make history. Amazon Projects team need to have in-depth understanding of Project life cycle including span allocation for all the four pillars (assessment, design and detailing, Execution and Performance qualification) of any project. Some of the key aspects of the role are Site Selection along with the RE team, Project statutory and Legal compliances adherence, Project Safety both in terms of design and execution, value management for large and medium scale projects, executing various formats of technical projects-green field, brown field and Improvement projects.


Key job responsibilities
The candidate is required to lead the planning and execution of all building formats and will be for a specific region - North India. The Project Engineer role will be responsible for setting up new Centers across different business formats, capacity expansion of existing facilities as well as relocation of facilities as per business requirements.

Project Manager will be responsible for the on time and safe delivery and hand over of facilities across business formats as listed above, ensuring that the project is executed with highest standards and good engineering practices. Other roles and responsibilities of the Project Manager will include
- Support RE Team in site visits and due diligence through site inspections for a feasibility assessment of setting up a facility for the given business format
- To work on finalizing the conceptual layouts and to assess the layout parameters from an operations perspective
- Preparing Developer’s Scope of Work for the site as per standards and specification
- Preparing Amazon scope of work and the cost estimate and preparation & submission of Build out Capex request for approval.
- Finalization of Project Management Consultants
- Project resource planning and management through the appointed PMC or hired resources
- Work with Procurement team to evaluate and finalize vendors for the projects
- Reviewing the material requirement with all stakeholders for setting up and Launching the facility and tracking till receipt and implementation.
- Progress monitoring and Execution management of onsite activities of Amazon scope of work
- Tracking and ensuring quality of work by the Developer
- Ensure a snag-free handover to Facilities Team
- Work with Launch team in preparation of the Launch of a site closer to completion
- Ensure budget adherence and safe execution at site - with no budget overruns and with a targeted Zero LTI
- Preparation of weekly progress reviews and reports with status updates
- Ensure timely project closure including the commercials

jobs.description:
Amazon Logistics (AMZL) is searching for a PE Innovation Engineer to take the AMZL Process Engineering Innovation team to the next level. Our logistics teams are changing the way we interact with customers around the globe every single day and solving some of the biggest logistical challenges facing not just Amazon, but the entire industry.

We are looking for an experienced, customer-obsessed Engineer to join the Amazon Logistics WW Process Engineering Team. This Engineer will be responsible for driving change, large-scale business communications, mitigating risks to plan, goal tracking, data delivery and governance, process improvement, and other ad-hoc projects that drive efficiency and reduce risk across the PE Innovation portfolio for Process Engineering. The right candidate can influence without authority and present to senior leadership. They will be a strong advocate for the team and will work hand-in-hand with key stakeholders in developing and growing strategic relationships, both internally and externally, as well as creating and streamlining internal processes. The Engineer will strive for service excellence, and drive centralization and adoption of our mechanisms with all global stakeholders.

This highly visible, cross-functional role will require effective collaboration and efficient communication with multiple teams (Product, Tech, Program, Finance, Legal, SMEs, Local Ops, GP, etc.) and global stakeholders. The right candidate is proactive in understanding and communicating critical business needs, and knows how those translate into solutions. This person must have experience building long-term, strategic relationships, and becoming influential partners. This position requires heavy verbal and written communication on status, risks and our sourcing pipeline.

This role is a great fit for a professional team-player who is flexible, and very comfortable with ambiguity. The successful applicant will merge a strong technical depth with bar raising communication skills. The Engineer will possess a strong business and project management background, and is able to thrive and succeed in an entrepreneurial environment, and not be hindered by opposing priorities. They will not only successfully develop and drive programs across teams, but is willing to roll up their sleeves, dive into the weeds to get the job done, focusing on the data and details to ensure the right inputs are top of mind for our customers and stakeholders.
To be successful in this role you will need to be able to influence and work with senior leaders across multiple organizations and geographies. You will work with organizations such as Worldwide (WW) Engineering and Design, WW Tech SME, Last Mile Delivery and Tech as well as Operations to innovate at scale and drive the development roadmap.



Key job responsibilities
• Handle a high volume of requests, engagements and interactions in fast paced environment
• Track existing and set up new metrics as required to validate vendor and team performance
• Develop and implement performance improvement plans, working cross-functionally with internal and external stakeholders
• Report on performance in weekly and monthly forums with partner teams and senior leadership
• Set measurable targets that align with the overall goals of the organization
• Comprehend the technical requirements of our internal customers and become an expert in field
• Influencer: Innovative pioneer with the ability to identify opportunities and influence organizations to gain support and overcome resistance with data and persuasion.
• Significant experience overseeing partner teams through good judgment, making and data.
• Doer: Ability to successfully deliver end-to-end technology and vendor operations projects, working through the many obstacles along the way
• Communicator: Ability to present to senior leadership, communicate expectations and requirements equally well with business and technology teams, and capacity to write well-reasoned and data-oriented proposals, performing your own data examination as necessary
• Problem Solver: Ability to utilize exceptional problem-solving skills to work through difficult tasks

jobs.company_name: Servicios Comerciales Amazon Mexico S. de R.L. de C.V.
jobs.description:
We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated software engineer to join our team in the commercial real estate industry. In this role, you will work at a global scale to solve real-world challenges that will transform Amazon’s approach to Industrial Real Estate in ways we can’t even image yet. You will by applying tools in workflow automation, geospatial intelligence and mapping, and artificial intelligence. You will work closely with a team of experts to develop and implement innovative technology solutions that will revolutionize the way our stakeholders conduct business.

This is a fantastic opportunity for a strong software engineer looking to make a significant impact in the commercial real estate industry. You will have the chance to work on innovative technology solutions that will transform the way our clients do business. We offer competitive compensation, comprehensive benefits, and a dynamic work environment where you will be challenged and rewarded for your contributions. We invent every day. We are Global Realty Technologies and we will give you the tools and support you need to invent with us in ways that are rewarding, fulfilling and fun. If you are a strong developer with a passion for innovation, we encourage you to apply today.

A day in the life
- Lead, mentor, challenge and grow other software engineers on the team
- Develop and implement software solutions that leverage workflow automation, geospatial intelligence and mapping, and artificial intelligence to improve our clients' business processes
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy innovative software solutions that meet our clients' needs
- Actively engage and participate in software architecture and design reviews
- Maintain and enhance existing software applications to ensure they meet the evolving needs of the business
- Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code that adheres to industry best practices
- Participate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback to peers
- Stay up-to-date with emerging technologies and industry trends to ensure our software solutions remain at the forefront of the industry

About the team
Global Realty Tech manages software tools to centralize data about what buildings Amazon launches. Since inception, these tools have grown to encompass Amazon's worldwide operations (outside physical stores): we now cover thousands of active new launches, retrofit, and lease-related projects. We create global standards for managing data related to these capital projects.

Inclusive team culture
At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust

Work Life Balance
We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

Mentorship& Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.
